Review Codex 3.0 by OpenAI?

Reviewers describe Codex 3.0 as a strong autonomous coding agent that reads real codebases, works across files, and tests its own changes in a sandbox, making it especially trusted for larger chunks of implementation. Users say it is more dependable than some rivals that guess at project structure, though slower, with weak progress visibility and some context loss on bigger repos. Founders of MindPal and Velo also say it helps their teams ship faster and build products quickly.

### LLMs [...] Romain Batlle

used Cline to build MakeHub.ai

•3 reviews

Cline is simply the best AI coding agent out there. Part of my team's job is to test them all, and the Cline / Roo paradigm is great, and has been shaping the industry for the last 6 months, hence why Claude Code and other are copying them. The single alternative is Claude Code, but you can only use their. models, which are not currently SOTA, and it would be a mistake to bet that they will be SOTA all the time in the future.

results.title: The best AI coding agents in 2026 - Product Hunt
results.content:
## Top reviewed AI coding agents

Filter 

Top reviewed

"Across the most-reviewed AI coding agents, the market splits between deep in-editor engineering, terminal-native automation, and prompt-first app creation. Cursor leads on repo-aware editing, refactors, reviews, and multi-file work inside an IDE; Claude Code emphasizes command execution and test-driven changes from the terminal; Lovable targets rapid full-stack MVPs with visual editing, hosting, and GitHub-backed iteration."
